_comment: MIGRATION TARGET, not yet wired in. In v0 the correlation rules live as functions in src/Invoke-SecondOpinion.ps1 (see DESIGN.md). This file documents the schema each rule will move to so the engine becomes data-driven (the moat: editable by the maintainer or an LLM after each real fix, no code change).

ruleSchema.culprit: human label shown as the node title
ruleSchema.tierClass: one of: drive | gpu | memory | storage | cpu | driver | software | power | app
ruleSchema.when: predicate over collected signals (counts, codes, statuses)
ruleSchema.whatWouldChangeThis: what evidence would move the verdict
ruleSchema.searchTemplate: google/AI query seeded with the specific code/module

examples.confirmBy: Clean-reinstall the GPU driver with DDU, or roll back to the pre-incident version.
examples.whatWouldChangeThis: If crashes persist after a clean driver reinstall, suspect the GPU hardware or PSU.

examples.confirmBy: Remove any CPU/RAM overclock or XMP/EXPO profile; verify temperatures and PSU.
examples.whatWouldChangeThis: Stability after disabling overclock points at an unstable profile rather than failing silicon.
